steps.title: Read the shown graph
steps.content: The graph shown is $y=f(x)+19$. It slopes downward, so $f$ also has a negative slope because vertical shifts do not change slope.

steps.title: Undo the vertical shift
steps.content:
The shown graph has $y$-intercept $(0,3)$. To get $y=f(x)$, shift down $19$ units, giving a $y$-intercept of
$$
3-19=-16
$$
So $f$ has a negative intercept.

steps.title: Match the signs
steps.content: Since $c$ and $d$ are positive, $-d-cx$ has a negative intercept and a negative slope. The answer is A.
